mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns exceed +0.5% in > 60% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of > 1% over two months coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Breaching the 235.0 threshold in the consumer price index triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 235.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting higher wage growth and consumer spending that lifts equity markets over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue baselines and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 increases fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the index of industrial production below 95.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, forcing immediate equity deleveraging and negative returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns exceed +0.3% in > 60% of instances when INDPRO breaches the 95.0 threshold.
